topics.title: Day 1: Individual Development Plan
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 1: Individual Development Plan is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Create IDP
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 1: Individual Development Plan and its role in Learning plan, cert roadmap, goals
topics.interviewQuestion: What is an Individual Development Plan (IDP)?
topics.interviewAnswer: IDP aligns personal growth with career goals. Sections: career vision (5-year goal), current skills assessment, development needs, action plan (12 months), resources needed, success measures, review dates. Focus on 2-3 key development areas per year. Share with manager for organizational alignment.

quiz.question: What is the time horizon for career vision?
How many development areas per year?
Who should review the IDP?
What follows current skills assessment?
What are success measures?
Which best describes Day 1: Individual Development Plan?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Individual Development Plan is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of Day 1: Individual Development Plan?
quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 1: Individual Development Plan typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

quiz.question: Where does Day 1: Individual Development Plan apply in real-world scenarios?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Individual Development Plan concepts are universal across all security domains.

quiz.question: Which certification exam covers Day 1: Individual Development Plan?
quiz.explanation: Most major certifications touch on Day 1: Individual Development Plan at some level.

quiz.question: Best way to gain hands-on experience with Day 1: Individual Development Plan?
quiz.explanation: Practical application in a home lab is the most effective way to build skills. Reading alone is not enough.

quiz.question: Which skill is NOT directly related to Day 1: Individual Development Plan?
quiz.explanation: While web development may use security concepts, Day 1: Individual Development Plan is more directly tied to risk, response, and monitoring.

quiz.question: Scenario: You encounter Day 1: Individual Development Plan during a security audit. What is your first step?
quiz.explanation: A professional approach to Day 1: Individual Development Plan during an audit is to identify the issue and document findings for further analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common tool used with Day 1: Individual Development Plan?
quiz.explanation: Depending on context, Day 1: Individual Development Plan can involve firewalls, SIEMs, or packet analyzers.

quiz.question: Understanding Day 1: Individual Development Plan helps prevent which type of attack?
quiz.explanation: The specific attack Day 1: Individual Development Plan prevents depends on its context within the security domain.

quiz.question: How does Day 1: Individual Development Plan relate to the CIA triad?
quiz.explanation: Most security concepts including Day 1: Individual Development Plan can impact all three pillars of the CIA triad.

topics.title: Day 2: Certification Roadmap
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 2: Certification Roadmap is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Plan certification path
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 2: Certification Roadmap and its role in Learning plan, cert roadmap, goals
topics.interviewQuestion: What is a strategic certification roadmap?
topics.interviewAnswer: Foundation (year 1): Security+, Network+, CySA+. Intermediate (year 2-3): GCIH, AWS Security, OSCP, CISSP associate. Advanced (year 3-5): CISSP, CISM, SANS GIAC, cloud security certs. Expert (5+): ISSAP, ISSEP, CISSP-ISSMP. Sequence matters: build from broad to specialized.

quiz.question: What is a foundation cert?
What is an intermediate cert?
What is an advanced cert?
What should drive cert choices?
What is the recommended sequence?

topics.title: Day 3: Time Management for Learning
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 3: Time Management for Learning is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Manage learning time
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 3: Time Management for Learning and its role in Learning plan, cert roadmap, goals
topics.interviewQuestion: How do you manage time for continuous learning?
topics.interviewAnswer: Block dedicated time: 30 min daily + 2-4 hours weekly. Calendar blocks with recurring events. Use Pomodoro (25 min focused, 5 min break). Priority matrix: urgent/important quadrant. Protect learning time like any commitment. Track hours toward goals. Use commute, lunch, waiting time for passive learning (podcasts, videos).

quiz.question: What is recommended daily learning time?
What is the Pomodoro technique?
What helps during commute?
What quadrant is learning?
How should learning time be treated?

topics.title: Day 4: Mentorship and Coaching
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 4: Mentorship and Coaching is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Find mentorship
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 4: Mentorship and Coaching and its role in Learning plan, cert roadmap, goals
topics.interviewAnswer: Find mentors: internal (senior team members), external (LinkedIn, professional orgs), peer (same level mutual support). Prepare: specific goals, questions, agenda for each meeting. Follow up: implement advice, report results. Multiple mentors for different areas: technical, career, leadership. Consider reverse mentoring (junior teaches senior).

quiz.question: What type of mentor helps with career strategy?
How should mentor meetings be prepared?
options: Specific agenda with goals and questions

quiz.question: What is reverse mentoring?
How many mentors should you have?
What should you do after mentor advice?

topics.title: Day 5: Personal Project Portfolio
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 5: Personal Project Portfolio is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Build project portfolio
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 5: Personal Project Portfolio and its role in Learning plan, cert roadmap, goals
topics.interviewQuestion: How do you build a cybersecurity portfolio?
topics.interviewAnswer: GitHub: detection rules (Sigma), automation scripts, analysis tools, CTF write-ups. Blog: technical deep-dives, tutorials, incident walkthroughs. YouTube: lab builds, tool demos. Documentation: portfolio website or PDF with project summaries, screenshots, results. Quality over quantity: 3-5 polished projects better than 20 shallow ones.

quiz.question: What is a good portfolio project?
options: Sigma detection rule for specific attack

quiz.question: What makes a good CTF write-up?
How many polished projects to showcase?
What demonstrates capability to employers?
options: GitHub with security projects and contributions

quiz.question: What blog format is most effective?
options: Technical deep-dives with code and diagrams
